items.title: Perfect World Games Collaborates with Samsung Electronics to Launch New Galaxy Special Theme
items.summary: Source: Perfect World ©Perfect WorldPerfect World Games announced a collaboration with Samsung Electronics to release a special theme for the 'new Galaxy Z series' featuring '...
items.content:
Perfect World Games announced a collaboration with Samsung Electronics to release a special theme for the 'new Galaxy Z series' featuring 'Neverness to Everness,' a new urban open world RPG developed by Hotta Studio, a subsidiary of Perfect World.

This collaboration was launched to commemorate the release of Samsung Electronics' next-generation flagship foldable phones: the Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Fold8, and Flip8.

The 'Neverness to Everness' Mint special theme is inspired by 'Mint,' a bright and friendly core member of the Paranormal Bureau's Containment Team 2. The theme captures Mint's vibrant and lovely personality through an emotional design integrated across the smartphone, including the lock screen, icons, wallpapers, and key app interfaces.

This special theme is offered as an exclusive benefit for customers who purchase the new Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Fold8, or Flip8. Customers in Korea who purchase and activate these devices can download the theme by visiting the special event page within the Galaxy Store. The download period runs from August 4 to October 31.

An offline event where users can also obtain the special theme will be held at five Samsung Store locations nationwide: Hongdae, Daejeon, Sangmu, Shinsegae Daegu, and Shinsegae Centum City.

Visitors can download the special theme by tagging their Galaxy smartphone to an NFC terminal on-site; this event runs from July 23 to August 3. Notably, on the first day of the event, July 23, a limited quantity of 300 Galaxy Store discount coupons worth ₩20k will be distributed per store.

The centerpiece of this collaboration, 'Echocalypse' (이환), is a supernatural urban fantasy open world RPG that has gained significant popularity among global subculture gamers for its blend of free-roaming urban exploration and diverse life content, such as decorating one's own personal space. With its recent 1.2 version major update, the game introduced a new character, 'Shinku,' and a new anomaly board game mode titled '999-Day Night Tale,' continuing to expand the unique appeal of the game through consistent updates.

Meanwhile, the newly released Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra features an even wider large screen and an optimized display ratio, providing a comfortable experience for the game's beautiful, vibrant 3D graphics and dynamic open world exploration. This allows users to take in the intricate details of 'Hetero City,' where supernatural 'anomalies' coexist, with a wide field of view, promising a more immersive and comfortable gameplay experience.

Detailed information regarding the 'Neverness to Everness' Mint special theme can be found on the Galaxy Store event page and the official website for 'Neverness to Everness'.

K/DA and Taric - Coa, Haeun, Yeovlynn, Rakang, Bong

Creative use of ZEPETO - Abigelic Cosplay Spot

Perfect World Games and Hotta Studio are rolling out Phase 2 of Neverness to Everness (NTE) Version 1.2 on July 29, 2026. Titled "999 Nights," this second half brings the urban open-world RPG's first dedicated limited S-Class healer to limited banners, expanding character choices alongside new story updates across Hethereau.

Iroi headlines the update as an S-Class Anima hybrid support. Designed as a top-tier sustain unit, she plugs a major gap in NTE’s lineup by delivering team-wide healing, crowd control, and damage buffs built around Blossom-reaction team comps... making her a natural partner for Cosmos DPS carries like Shinku. Her toolkit centers on Imagination, a unique resource that boosts both her offensive output and support skills. She also brings a Regression passive, allowing teammates to avoid lethal damage by instantly swapping places with one of her three summons.

Her limited banner, "The Lifeline," runs from July 29 through August 19. Unlike many gacha titles, NTE skips the standard 50/50 system entirely. Banner progression directly guarantees the featured S-Class character without off-banner spooks, capping out at a 90-pull hard pity.

Iroi takes over for Phase 1's martial artist DPS Shinku, whose "Before the Dawn" banner ends when maintenance hits on July 29. Players can also target Iroi's signature Arc equipment on a concurrent weapon banner during Phase 2.

Alongside the new banner, Version 1.2 continues the Scarborough Fair event line, diving deeper into the strange anomalies popping up around Hethereau. The patch also brings the 999 Nights tabletop-inspired mode on the Warren Continent, plus the new Draco motorcycle... which features exclusive synergy effects when paired with Shinku's signature outfit.

Looking ahead, Hotta Studio is already dropping hints for Version 1.3, which should land around late August given the game's typical six-week cycle. Early teasers point to the climax of the "Scarlet Letter" arc, introducing former antagonist Zankou as a playable Incantation S-Class unit alongside a new Anima character, Linko. Until then, players grinding high-level Abyss floors can pick up Iroi to bolster squad survival before the next story chapter lands.

items.title: Neverness to Everness brought developers hundreds of millions of dollars - PC and Android lead
items.summary: Neverness to Everness by Perfect World Games earned over $207 million in two months after its launch on April 29, 2026.
items.content:
0 Источник: Automaton Media 20 Jul 11:43 Neverness to Everness by Perfect World Games earned over $207 million in two months after its launch on April 29, 2026.

Neverness to Everness brought developers over 1.4 billion yuan or $207 million in two months after its release.

The Perfect World Games title launched on April 29, 2026. On the first day, sales exceeded 100 million yuan or $14.8 million, and a month later, global revenue surpassed the figures for the Chinese market.

According to the company, over 60% of NTE's revenue came from PC and Android players. The iOS version has repeatedly topped the charts for the most profitable apps in China and beyond, and the game also led the PlayStation Store charts in some countries, including Japan.

At the same time, Perfect World Games expects a loss for the first half of the 2026 fiscal year of 80–120 million yuan ($12–18 million). The reason is large marketing expenses before the global open beta test in April. The company expects that profits from NTE will begin to be accounted for from the third quarter.

Currently, Neverness to Everness's revenue accounts for about 25% of Perfect World Games' total gaming division revenue for 2025.

The game is available on Android, iOS, PS5, Windows, and macOS.

items.title: Shadow-n-Seek: Gameplay Guide
items.summary: The all-new asymmetric PvP mode Shadow-n-Seek is now live! During each match, you'll be randomly assigned to the Hider or Hunter side for a tense, thrilling showdown of disguise and pursuit. Duration July 17, 10:00 - August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8
items.content:
The all-new asymmetric PvP mode Shadow-n-Seek is now live! During each match, you'll be randomly assigned to the Hider or Hunter side for a tense, thrilling showdown of disguise and pursuit.

Duration

July 17, 10:00 - August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

The Two Sides

🎭 Hider: When the match begins, you get a prep window to explore the map and pick one of three items to disguise yourself. Once transformed, you look just like an ordinary object in the scene, making you tough for Hunters to spot! You can't attack while hidden, so rely on moving, repositioning, and planting decoys to stay out of the Hunters' hands.

🔍 Hunter: You have 2 minutes and 20 seconds to track down and catch every Hider! Hunters have a scanning ability; land a hit on a Hider to trigger the catch. But be careful: swing at the wrong target and the backlash costs you HP, so look closely before you strike!

Win Conditions

Hiders win: at least 2 Hiders are still alive when the timer runs out, or all Hunters' HP is depleted.

Hunters win: catch every Hider before time runs out.

Event Rewards

Take part in the event to earn the event currency "Paper Cup Phone", then visit the Shadebound Shop to redeem rewards like the "Gotcha!" avatar frame, Annulith, Floe Cinema Ticket, and Fons.

items.title: “Each ability feels like it could have been the central mechanic of its own game.” We ask a fellow developer what makes NTE: Neverness to Everness so impressive
items.summary: Indie-Us-Games CEO Masahiko Nakamura talks about the the strengths of NTE: Neverness to Everness from a dev's perspective.
items.content:
Home Game Development “Each ability feels like it could have been the central mechanic of its own game.” We ask a fellow developer what makes NTE: Neverness to Everness so impressive Indie-Us-Games CEO Masahiko Nakamura talks about the the strengths of NTE: Neverness to Everness from a dev's perspective.

Column , Game Development 2026-07-09 22:00 AUTOMATON WEST 2 Earlier this year, Perfect World Games released NTE: Neverness to Everness (hereafter NTE), a free-to-play open-world action RPG where players track down and contain supernatural “Anomalies” scattered across an urban setting. The game comes with a colorful cast of playable characters who, beyond differing appearances and stats, offer unique abilities like gravity manipulation or skill copying, fundamentally changing the way combat and exploration play out.

The title has also attracted attention for its vast cityscapes built in Unreal Engine 5 and reality-warping Anomaly scenes. Hoping to learn more about what makes NTE impressive from a technical point of view, AUTOMATON Japan recently spoke with Masahiko Nakamura, technical artist and head of Indie-us Games, a studio specializing in Unreal Engine development. In this installment, we focus on the technical ingenuity behind NTE’s character abilities and visual effects.

—I believe one of NTE’s stand-out features is its unique character abilities, but was there anything that stood out to you technically in this respect?

Nakamura: The signature abilities of the featured characters so far, such as Nanally’s gravity manipulation, Xun’s time stop, and Lacrimosa’s copy ability, have really stood out to me.

I think these are the kind of core mechanics that, in any other game, would be considered the “selling point” which the whole game is built upon. And yet, in NTE, they’re all introduced as individual abilities of specific characters.

What’s more, not only are features like these difficult to implement, but they also come with extremely high debugging costs. For example, Nanally’s gravity control isn’t particularly extraordinary in itself, given that Unreal Engine already has a Custom Gravity feature, but NTE’s world is packed with objects, and players have a great deal of freedom to move around. Once you start changing the direction of gravity in that kind of environment, it’s impossible to predict what all could happen.

In fact, there was actually a bug that caused players to be launched into the air due to gravity behaving incorrectly, which got patched out in an update. I imagine the developers fully expected issues like that to crop up. Even so, the game gives me the impression that the team went, “It’s fun, so let’s do it, bugs be damned.” I find it impressive that they made a feature that’s inherently so trouble-prone one of the game’s stand-out mechanics.

—Do you think the high risk of bugs would make many developers shy away from that kind of feature?

Nakamura: Yes. Even if you can anticipate problems, the cost of actually debugging them can be astronomical. But with NTE, I get the feeling they’ve simply resigned themselves to that reality (laughs) .

The time-stop ability is another feature that can be a headache. In Unreal Engine, things running on the CPU are relatively easy to “pause,” but effects running on the GPU aren’t. If you stop time in NTE, you’ll notice that the wind and ocean waves continue moving, but rain and snow do not. The developers cleverly create an illusion of stopped time by removing the original particle effects and swapping in separate ones showing static raindrops or snowflakes.

—That sounds like something only a fellow engineer would notice.

Nakamura: Most players probably wouldn’t think twice about it, but from a developer’s perspective, the difference between what’s moving and what’s stopped is quite stark. If QA filed a bug saying, “This doesn’t stop when time freezes,” I’d probably respond, “I really don’t want to fix that” because of how much work it is (laughs).

Lacrimosa’s copy ability is also an ambitious feature. It basically works like Kirby’s copy ability, which means the more enemy types you add, the more abilities you have to implement. Every new enemy requires another unique ability, so it essentially creates an endless workload.

items.title: NTE: Neverness to Everness Has Some Trouble On Steam Deck
items.summary: NTE: Neverness to Everness may be on Steam now, but it falls into the same performance issues on Steam Deck that most similar gacha games encounter.
items.content:
Game Settings Nintendo Reviews News Tips and Guides Other Reviews Accessory Reviews Handheld Reviews About Us jQuery('#-pro-menu-237-7 .oxy-pro-menu-show-dropdown .menu-item-has-children > a', 'body').each(function(){ jQuery(this).append(' '); }); jQuery('#-pro-menu-237-7 .oxy-pro-menu-show-dropdown .menu-item:not(.menu-item-has-children) > a', 'body').each(function(){ jQuery(this).append(' '); }); Search for: Opinion

If you haven't yet, follow us on X (Twitter) , Mastodon , or Bluesky to know when we post new articles, and join our Reddit or Subscribe to us on YouTube to check out our content and interact with our awesome community. Thank you for supporting us!

I’m not sure why, but I’ve been on a gacha game kick lately. I got back into Wuthering Waves and found myself turning my AYN Odin 3 into a Gacha game machine. I downloaded Genshin Impact, Honkai Star Rail, Zenless Zone Zero, and a new one I’ve been playing more of, NTE: Neverness to Everness . It’s been a lot of fun to play, and it was perfect timing because the game just recently launched on Steam. With that in mind, I wanted to check it out and see how the game is running on the Steam Deck and how the experience compares to the standalone version that we can manually install via the game’s launcher.

For those who may not know, NTE: Neverness to Everness is a gacha game created by Hotta Studio. We run around in an urban open-world city, taking on missions, getting into flashy combat, and collecting new characters through its gacha systems. There’s a lot more to it that makes me think of GTA or Marvel’s Spider-Man world than we can get cars to drive around the city quickly or fast travel. It seems very F2P-friendly as well, with lots of unlockables for free and a lot of chances to get the characters we want. Just recently, with the 1.2 update, the team gave out a free character and about 2200 of their premium currency, which is just a treat.

However, performance on the Steam Deck isn’t always the greatest for these kinds of games. A lot of them are very CPU-heavy, which leads to a good chunk of stutters that can harm the experience. Almost every gacha game I’ve played has similar problems, and that includes NTE .

In contained fights, on the lowest possible settings, it can stick to 30 FPS for the most part, though it does stutter and drop here and there when a lot of flashy effects are on the screen. So, some combat scenarios are a bit worse than others. However, running around the city can cost some drops, and driving around is significantly worse. So, I would consider it somewhat playable for minor grinding, but it’s definitely going to have its issues when trying to get around without fast traveling or in large fights.

I also took some time to compare the Steam and Launcher versions of the game. Usually, Steam versions will have slightly better performance and fewer stutters due to the shared shader cache system on Steam. However, it is a fairly new release, so the cache isn’t there yet. Because of this, performance is relatively the same, with the Steam version performing slightly worse. As we can see in the visual below, I tested both games with the same “Smooth” preset on the Fourth Stop of the Gloaming Circle Ghost Train with the same exact characters, and the results are pretty similar to each other.

Ultimately, it’s possible that within the next week, we will see the shader cache appear, and performance will get better, especially when it comes to stutters. However, it’s still going to need those compromises, including lower settings and a 30 FPS cap for stability, and we probably will still have some CPU bottlenecks that result in other stutters, especially when driving around. I do see some possibilities to play on the Steam Deck this way for some of its content, but it’s not something that’ll be stable throughout the entire game.

NTE: Neverness to Everness is currently free to play on Steam right now.

If you enjoyed this article, check out the rest of the content on SteamDeckHQ ! We have a wide variety of game reviews and news that will help your gaming experience. Whether you're looking for news , tips and tutorials , game settings and reviews , or just want to stay up-to-date on the latest trends, we've got you covered!

items.title: NTE: Neverness to Everness released on Steam - works on SteamOS / Linux with a workaround
items.summary: NTE: Neverness to Everness is the latest bigger Anime gacha game to arrive on Steam from Perfect World Games / Hotta Studio and it can work on SteamOS / Linux.
items.content:
NTE: Neverness to Everness is the latest bigger Anime gacha game to arrive on Steam from Perfect World Games / Hotta Studio and it can work on SteamOS / Linux.

Like many other Anime gacha games it uses Anti-Cheat Expert (ACE), which by default seems to have blocks in place but we can get it to work thanks to a community-made version of Proton.

You'll need the latest DW-Proton for it to work, otherwise with Valve's official Proton the game will just instantly quit after you hit the Play button.

After that, you'll be good to go and the game will launch properly. A bit of a nuisance, but this is the same story for multiple similar Anime games.

Hopefully this will get you up and running in no time! With DW-Proton it worked well for me on Fedora KDE.

Our anti-cheat page has been updated to note this status.

NTE: Neverness to Everness Release Date: 📅 7th July 2026 Platform: ⚛ Proton / Wine Devs: Hotta Studio , Perfect World Games Official links: Steam

items.title: Neverness to Everness launches on Steam and Epic Games Store with Version 1.2 update
items.summary: Players can also claim a free exclusive in-game item as part of the game's platform expansion celebration.
items.content:
All News Neverness to Everness is now available on Steam and Epic Games Store (Image: Hotta Studio).

{" "} Neverness to Everness launches on Steam and Epic Games Store with Version 1.2 update .iframe-media {width: 100%;aspect-ratio: auto 16/9;} .img-media {width: 100%;aspect-ratio: auto 16/9;} .a-img-media {aspect-ratio: auto 16/9;} Players can also claim a free exclusive in-game item as part of the game's platform expansion celebration. Hotta Studio's Neverness to Everness is now officially available on the major PC platforms Steam and Epic Games Store, as well as Samsung's Galaxy Store on mobile platforms. NTE is also giving players a free exclusive in-game avatar frame to celebrate its platform expansion.

The game's arrival to Steam and the Epic Games Store also comes alongside the major Version 1.2 update, which introduces the new S-Class character Shinku, a new main story chapter, another exclusive vehicle obtainable through a draw event, and plenty of additional content.

Platforms: PC (via official launcher, Steam, and Epic Games Store), PlayStation 5, and mobile platforms (iOS and Android)

More than a month after its global launch, the supernatural gacha game, Neverness to Everness, has finally arrived on Steam and the Epic Games Store. It has also launched on the Galaxy Store, giving mobile players another option to download the game from. Previously, NTE was only available through its standalone PC launcher, PlayStation 5, and mobile’s App Store and Google Play.

To celebrate the expansion, Neverness to Everness offers the exclusive "Celebration Fireworks" in-game avatar frame for free. Players can grab the free item by redeeming the code WELCOMETONTE between 8 July and 18 August.

Once the redemption period ends, the code will no longer be valid. After redeeming it, be sure to claim the reward from your in-game Mail before it expires.

Yes, your progress in Neverness to Everness carries across all supported platforms, including Steam and Epic Games, provided you sign in with the same account using your linked email address and password. This means you can switch to Steam without losing your save data or starting over.

Sarah "KZ" Zulkiflee Sarah “KZ” Zulkiflee writes for GosuGamers, where she channels a long-standing obsession with Dota 2 into her work. While still often lost in the depths of MMR grinding, she has been actively experimenting with games across every genre imaginable. Six years into her career, she still wonders how she miraculously ended up in her dream industry. Anyway, she’s just happy to be here. Gaming Hasbro reveals The Legend of Zelda figures based on Tears of the Kingdom, launching in 2027

Gaming Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Easter egg seems to tease Assassin's Creed Hexe

Should you pull for Remielle, Sigrid, Aria, Yuzuha, Dialyn, or Harumasa in Zenless Zone Zero 3.1?

items.title: Version 1.2 "999 Nights" Patch Notes
items.summary: Hey Appraiser, NTE will undergo version maintenance on July 8, 2026 from 06:00 to 11:00 (UTC+8). Once it's done, the all-new Version 1.2 "999 Nights" goes live. "Pink Paws Heist" will be temporarily unavailable starting at 05:30 (UTC+8) and
items.content:
Hey Appraiser,

NTE will undergo version maintenance on July 8, 2026 from 06:00 to 11:00 (UTC+8). Once it's done, the all-new Version 1.2 "999 Nights" goes live. "Pink Paws Heist" will be temporarily unavailable starting at 05:30 (UTC+8) and will reopen once maintenance wraps up.

Login will be unavailable during this period, so please plan accordingly. Since this update is on the larger side, we recommend using Wi-Fi or mobile data to complete the download. We apologize for any inconvenience!

※ The "Forsaken Path" Limited Board ends on July 8, 2026, at 05:59 (UTC+8). Be sure to head to the Fogshroud Perks page and redeem your rewards before then.

\

Create a character before Version 1.2 ends to receive an S-Class Standard Character Selection Box.

Duration: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Distribution: Sent via in-game mail after maintenance. Remember to claim the items before they expire.

※ Open the S-Class Standard Character Selection Box to choose any one of these characters to add to your roster: Sakiri, Baicang, Hathor, Fadia, Daffodill, or Jiuyuan.

\

Since launch, we have received invaluable feedback from our Appraisers to help improve the game. We sincerely apologize for any current shortcomings and are actively working to optimize the overall experience in upcoming updates.

Thank you for your patience and continued support. As a token of our gratitude, we are distributing the following gift:

● Appreciation Gift: Annulith x1,600

● Delivery: Sent via in-game mail after the July 8, 2026 update.

● Eligibility: Appraisers who create a character and unlock the "Mail" feature before Version 1.2 ends.

\

● Duration: July 8, 2026, 06:00–11:00 (UTC+8)

● Maintenance Compensation: Annulith x300

● Eligibility: Appraisers who created their character before July 8, 2026, 06:00 (UTC+8)

● Distribution: Sent via in-game mail after maintenance. Remember to claim the items before they expire.

※ Maintenance may end earlier or later than scheduled depending on actual progress. Please keep an eye on our official announcements for updates.

● Bug Fix Compensation: Annulith x300

● Eligibility: Appraisers who created their character before July 8, 2026, 06:00 (UTC+8)

● Distribution: Sent via in-game mail after maintenance. Remember to claim the items before they expire.

\

PC: Close the game, reopen the launcher, and click Update.

Android: Launch the game and follow the in-game update prompt.

iOS: Download or update from the App Store.

Mac: Download or update from the App Store.

PlayStation®: Open the system menu, select NTE, press the OPTIONS button on your controller, choose Confirm Upgrade, then select Check for Update.

\

1. New Character & Arc

● New Exclusive S-Class Character: Shinku!

Available: July 8 (after update) – July 29, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Unlock Requirement: Complete the main story quest "Prologue - A New Destination - Head to Eibon Antique Shop"

Board Details: The "Before the Dawn" Limited Board features the exclusive S-Class character Shinku, along with A-Class characters Mint, Edgar, and Adler.

During the event, the "Before the Dawn" Limited Board also features the "Overcast Canopy" glider livery, the "Hidden Dragon" Blizzard-V4 livery, and Shinku's outfit "Student of Terrasea". Claim them from the Resolve Perks page by accumulating 50/120/200 rolls, or obtain them randomly through rolls before reaching these milestones.

*For full details, head to Scarborough Fair in-game.

● New Limited S-Class Arc: "Blushing Mirage"

Available: July 8 (after update) – July 29, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Unlock Requirement: Complete the main story quest "Prologue - A New Destination - Head to Eibon Antique Shop"

Research Guide: Spend Tri-Keys or Resolve Keys to participate in the "Resolve Special" Arc Research Program and obtain the limited S-Class Arc "Blushing Mirage". This Arc is currently only available through this program.

*For full details, head to Arc Shop › Arc Research Program in-game.

2. New Story

● New Main Story Episode: "Fighting with a Dragon"

Ready to go? Our fantastical adventure is about to begin!

Available: After the Version 1.2 update

Unlock Requirement: Complete the main story episode "Dreamwalk Corridor" and reach Hunter Lvl 26.

※ The new location Warren Continent can be reached during the main story episode "Fighting with a Dragon".

※ In response to the concerns many Appraisers have shared about the continuation of the "Dreamwalk Corridor" storyline, we'd like to clarify: this main story episode is told out of chronological order, using a flashback structure. It depicts a lighter, more relaxed chapter set after the dust has settled on the "Scarlet Letter" incident, once everyone has returned to their peaceful daily lives. A new fantasy adventure is about to begin. Dive in and have a blast.

● City Hangouts

A new City Hangouts story featuring Shinku.

Available: After the Version 1.2 update

Unlock Requirement: Obtain the character Shinku, or complete the main story episode "Fighting with a Dragon".

● Home Time

A new Invitation mode featuring Shinku.

Available: After the Version 1.2 update

Unlock Requirement: Obtain the character Shinku and reach the required Bond Level.

3. New Location

● New Location: Warren Continent (reachable during the main story episode "Fighting with a Dragon")

A vast land brimming with fantasy, with adventures, challenges, and opportunities hiding in every corner. What lies at the next crossroads?

4. New Outfits & Vehicle Liveries

● Shinku's new outfit "Dragonhunter" is now available in the Shop

Discount Period: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Discount Price: Riftcrystal x2,680

Original Price: Riftcrystal x3,280 (18% off, limited time)

Shop Availability: Permanent

● Shinku's new outfit "Dawn Patrol" is now available in the Shop

Discount Period: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Discount Price: Riftcrystal x1,680

Original Price: Riftcrystal x2,480 (32% off, limited time)

Shop Availability: Permanent

● Shinku's new outfit "Student of Terrasea"

How to Get: While the "Before the Dawn" Limited Board is active, roll on the board and land on the matching tile to obtain the outfit, or claim it from the Resolve Perks page once you've accumulated enough rolls.

● Shinku's new outfit "Labyrinth Lily"

How to Get: Unlocked after you obtain Shinku and reach Bond Lvl 10.

● Shinku's new outfit "Normie Heroine"

How to Get: Purchased from the "999 Nights" Button Shop.

● The Porsche 918 Spyder exclusive liveries "Tomato Dash" and "Rose & Violet" are now available in the Shop

Available: After the July 8 version update (UTC+8)

Price: Riftcrystal x980 or Fons x3,000,000

*Purchase using either Riftcrystal or Fons; only one option may be selected.

5. New Mystery Box Event "Neon Rift" Goes Live

Duration: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: Spend Fons or Annulith to open "Neon Rift" Mystery Boxes. The cost goes up with each box you open. There are 15 boxes in total, and opening all of them unlocks every reward in the pool.

Mystery Box Contents: The "Neon Rift" Mystery Boxes feature the limited vehicle "Draco", Esper Zero's exclusive outfit "Glory Days", Esper Zero's exclusive outfit colors "Glory Days - Starfall" and "Glory Days - Braveheart", Fabricated Dice x10, A Handwritten Letter, Fons x100,000, and the exclusive avatar frame "Ember Trail".

6. New Events

● "Circle Gifts" Login Event

Duration: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: Log in for a total of 7 days to earn Solid Dice x10.

● "999 Nights" Limited-Time Event

Duration: July 8 (after update) – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: The party's assembled! Ready to conquer all of Warren Continent! Take part during the event to earn rewards like Annulith, Fabricated Dice, Fons, Floe Cinema Tickets, Elite Hunter Guides, Chaotic Dye, and more.

● "Shadow-n-Seek" Limited-Time Event

Duration: July 17, 10:00 – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: One, two, three... don't move a muscle! During the event, take part to earn the event currency "Paper Cup Phone", then visit the Shadebound Shop to redeem rewards like the "Gotcha!" avatar frame, Annulith, Floe Cinema Tickets, Fons, and more.

● "Stamina Recharge" Limited-Time Event

Duration: July 13, 05:00 – July 20, 04:59 (server time)

Details: During the event, City Stamina is consumed at 2x efficiency in "Hethereau Hobbies" activities.

● "Gold Clash" Limited-Time Event

Duration: July 20, 05:00 – August 3, 04:59 (server time)

Details: During the event, earn Fons at 2x efficiency in "Pink Paws Heist".

7. Other Content

● "Eibon Partner Program" Now Live

Duration: July 8, 2026, 11:00 – August 19, 05:59 (UTC+8)

Details: During the event, Appraisers can earn rewards like Annulith by inviting Returning Appraisers back to the game.

Appraiser Share your invite code or invite link to bring back eligible Returning Appraisers, and earn base rewards plus Lucky Draw chances.

Once Returning Appraisers log in and finish the 3-day quests, they can also share their own invite code to bring other eligible Appraisers back and join the web event.

※ Returning Appraiser status can take a moment to update. If it hasn't refreshed, wait 2 to 4 minutes and reload the page.

● "999 Nights" Mini-Game

Available: After the Version 1.2 update

Details: Take part to earn "Mystery Buttons", then head to the Button Shop to redeem event rewards including Shinku's new outfit "Normie Heroine"; Esper Zero's outfits "Unassuming Warrior" and "The Blademaster"; Esper Zero's exclusive outfit colors "The Blademaster - Ocher", "The Blademaster - Morningstar", and "The Blademaster - Verdant"; Mint's outfits "Wild as the Wind", "Sylvan Hunter", and "Aurora Flame"; Mint's exclusive outfit colors "Sylvan Hunter - Ridge", "Sylvan Hunter - Lilac", "Sylvan Hunter - Bloomwhisper", "Aurora Flame - Shadeleaf", and "Aurora Flame - Dreammere"; Esper Zero's "Unassuming Warrior" dye orb set; Mint's "Wild as the Wind" dye orb set; the title "Dawn Warrior"; the namecard "Forever Fuzzy"; the avatar "Poet's Epilogue"; the avatar frame "Moonlit Dragon"; plus Annulith, Floe Cinema Tickets, Fons, and more.

● Version 1.2 "Circle Bounty" Guide

Available: July 8 (after update) – August 18, 23:59 (UTC+8)

Requirement: Complete the Main Quest "Relax Time"

Details: During the event, take on Circle Quests to earn Experience Points, raise your Circle Bounty Rank, and claim rewards like Fabricated Dice, Gold Carrota, De-noise Solution, A Handwritten Letter, and Expansion Cores.

Purchase Elite Hunter Supplies to unlock extra rewards as you rank up, including Annulith x680, Solid Dice x4, Fabricated Dice x2, Arc-light Trove x1, A Handwritten Letter x2, and Fons x700,000!

Purchase Honor Hunter Supplies to unlock all of the above rewards, plus the "Fuzzy" avatar, the "New Game+++" avatar frame, the "Summer Time" namecard, Fabricated Dice x5, A Handwritten Letter x1, and Fons x500,000!

\

◆ Add a teleport point at The Midnight Catzebo, where you can buy scratchers and scratch for prizes. Match the icons shown, and you win the prizes listed beneath them.

◆ Add an Outfit Recoloring feature for character outfits.

◆ Add Riichi Mahjong to Little Sparrow.

◆ Add a Fishkeeping feature.

◆ Add a Decor Blueprint feature.

◆ Add Bond quests for Shinku.

◆ Add Bond interactions for Shinku: reach the required Bond Level to unlock her passenger seat ride-along.

◆ Add text message conversations with Shinku.

◆ Add a combat tutorial for Shinku.

◆ Add Random Encounters for Iroi.

◆ Add the ability to display rare big fish on the back of your car.

◆ Add simplified controls for the Portable Player.

◆ Add a Player Reporting feature to Races - Online Battle mode.

◆ Add the ability to share Home Decor Blueprints.

◆ Raise the furniture Collection level cap and add one-tap collect. (If your Collection already went over the old cap before this update, the excess is now counted normally.)

◆ Improve Fight Club results scoring so knockouts and damage dealt count for more toward final rank and rewards.

◆ Add several new side quests.

◆ Reduce the difficulty of Anomaly Hunts at Appraisal Lvl 5 and above.

◆ Add long-press to bulk select upgrade materials when leveling up characters, Arcs, and Consoles.

◆ Add an option to hide parts and accessories on select outfits. (After this update, toggling Chaos's mask on or off moves from the G key to its own option in the Settings menu.)

◆ Convertible tops now stay down after your character gets out of the car.

◆ Add a semi-automatic throttle mode on mobile, toggleable in Settings.

◆ Add a Settings toggle for Arc special effects.

◆ Improve the elevator in the Pegasus Residence.

◆ Improvements to Bagel.

◆ Improve in-game avatar display.

◆ Improve controller support.

◆ Fix an issue where the quest log text could linger after completing Chaos's Encounter quest.

◆ Fix an issue where triggering an Anomaly Commission could break quest auto-tracking.

◆ Fix an issue where the new outfit notification dot could linger after switching your Appraiser's gender.

◆ Fix an issue where characters could get stuck at the table after leaving a Little Sparrow matchmaking room.

◆ Fix an issue where Hotori's Basic Attack button could disappear unexpectedly after casting her Ultimate.

◆ Fix an issue where the button to use the soap item fails to display properly in Fight Club when using a controller.

◆ Fix crashes that could occur in certain situations.

◆ Improve localization for several languages.

◆ Address various other known issues.

items.title: NTE is coming to Steam on July 8!
items.summary: Hey Appraisers, NTE officially launches on Steam on July 8. The Steam version supports cross-progression with existing platforms (mobile, PC, and more) across the Asia, America, Europe, and SEA servers, and launches straight into NTE's Vers
items.content:
Hey Appraisers,

NTE officially launches on Steam on July 8.

The Steam version supports cross-progression with existing platforms (mobile, PC, and more) across the Asia, America, Europe, and SEA servers, and launches straight into NTE's Version 1.2 content!

📺 Version 1.2 Preview Special Program

Follow this link to watch the livestream replay and learn about all the fun stuff coming in Version 1.2.

Wishlist it on Steam today, and start your Hethereau adventure on July 8!

The NTE Steam page is now live.

Wishlist now to stay updated on launch news.

items.title: Neverness to Everness has charmed me with its wacky cast but there's no way it becomes my gacha mainstay
items.summary: Offbeat humor and lovable characters aren’t enough to save this
items.content:
Offbeat humor and lovable characters aren’t enough to save this "anime GTA" from its lackluster open world.

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Hotta Studio) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 10 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter It was a quirky journey to find some tomatoes that lured me into believing that Neverness to Everness, the new sci-fi urban action game, could be my new favorite gacha. I had just bought my first cafe in its City Tycoon side activity, you see, and needed a few dozen veggies to start serving better meals. No problem, that nearby shop with a tomato sign out front must sell some. Somehow it didn't, which seemed weird, but I'm glad the shopkeeper was a big 'ol liar, because in my determination to hunt down some tomatoes, I went on quite the adventure.

Jessica Orr In search of a good open world gacha Are you a 3D gacha? Then I've played you. There's something about losing days of my life to a sprawling landscape that gives my brain that good feeling. Until it doesn't. Because apart from the grandaddy of open world gacha, Genshin Impact, the overworld in gachas tends to be underdeveloped, boring, or outright ignored. Which is why I was really hoping Neverness to Everness could come and save the day. Spoiler alert–it didn't. But it's not completely devoid of other charms.

On my way to the next shop I stop to talk to a mouthy pair of sentient boxing gloves. Might as well, right? Turns out it's a mini-boss who transforms the area into a boxing ring. Just one of the many quirky boss fights I can stumble into around the city. After that, I hop on my measly starting scooter and chug towards another shop. I can unlock flashier vehicles later (or steal them, GTA-style) but a scooter and its complimentary snail's pace is all I've got at the start. The next barrier between me and tomatoes is a line of green, bouncing ball… things blocking my path by minding their own business. These "anomalies" are the many weird and wonderful supernatural events (be they battles or friends) infesting the city. Think SCP, but with a Scooby Doo level of horror. Anomalies range from:

Onwards to another potential tomato dealer to get my side hustle as a cafe manager started, I'm distracted again, this time by a carjacking in progress. Being the good Hethereau city citizen that I am, I broke out the usual action-gacha classic of spamming flashy Ultimate and Skill moves to battle the perpetrators. NtE's real-time battles and the resulting lingo are nothing new for Genshin or Wuthering Waves players, but still feel satisfying, especially when you work in a well-timed dodge for bonus damage.

Then, finally , after returning the car to its owner, I found my new bud Basil selling tomatoes by a cherry blossom-covered roadside. It was only after all that I realized the item description had told me which shops sell tomatoes all along. A face palm moment from me for sure, but with reading comprehension skills I never would have had such a memorable gauntlet of goofy detours.

I've played 20 hours more of Neverness to Everness since tomato-gate and haven't had an experience like that again. What I thought was just the first of many surprise encounters in a vibrant city turned out to be the only example I've got. The fun I've had since has been in spite of the increasingly stale open world with its almost mandatory repeatable activities, and thanks entirely to its goofy main storyline and charming cast of characters.

Mixtape review: A gorgeous coming-of-age story that left me wondering why it's a videogame at all Neverness to Everness tier list and best characters for July 2026 Titanium Court review: An eccentric roguelite that's sometimes too random My new favorite goofballs

I'm a sucker for a silly plot, so I love that Neverness to Everness' quest design philosophy seems to have sprung from developer Hotta Studio asking "What if we made a game of anime filler episodes, but they were actually good?"

It starts off semi-serious with a sinister, mind-controlling UFO anomaly threatening the entire city, after which the main character (Zero) emerges with a classic case of anime amnesia. Hijinks soon kick in when they join Eibon: a perpetually-broke bunch of Anomaly Hunters led by lady Hitori, who would rather drink away what money she has than take part in a commission herself.

Keep up to date with the most important stories and the best deals, as picked by the PC Gamer team.

The rest of the team is made up of familiar archetypes: the impossibly polite butler-like Adler, brat kids Nanally and Sakiri, genius-child Edgar, and Hotori's stoic bodyguard, Daffodil. There's also Tageydo, an anomaly resembling a chubby otter with a TV in place of a head. They're a lovable little group of found family, which would be perfect if they didn't make Zero look so dull by comparison.

The commissions I complete with Eibon involve clearing out a haunted photography studio or helping Tageydo on a date, during which I'm bombarded with flashy battle set pieces, corny jokes, anime references galore, and increasingly goofy bosses. I've had to time jumps, dodges, and strikes to defeat a boombox; spar against a humungous, fluffy interdimensional dog; and battle with a floating ballgown who can slow down time around me. The boldest fights are saved for main missions and "Spinoff" side quests, but there are plenty of mini-bosses to test your combat skills in the city as well.

items.title: Neverness To Everness Review – How Much Game Can You Fit In A Game?
items.summary: Neverness To Everness has you in the shoes of an appraiser in the supernatural city of Hetherneau. Free-to-play game that might have too much to do.
items.content:
Neverness to Everness is a game that I have had my eye on for a while. I was able to play through two betas , and now I get the chance to review the official 1.0 launch of the game. Does the now-released version of the game pay off the build-up?

Game Name: Neverness To Everness Platform(s): PlayStation 5, PC, Mobile Devices Reviewed On: PlayStation 5 Developer(s): Hotta Studio Publisher(s): Perfect World Games Release Date: April 29th, 2026 Price: Free-To-Play

In Neverness to Everness or NTE, you will play as the Appraiser for Eibon Antiquities. An antique store that also hunts down anomalies from the supernatural. Your character will have an eye to see things that most other people can’t. You can see and hear the effects of anomalies, which is why you get hired as an appraiser. A lot of the factions you meet will also add depth to the city of Hethereau. This game is basically mashing a lot of games into each other, and the city itself is a sandbox reminiscent of Grand Theft Auto cities.

The game loop for Neverness to Everness is focused primarily on what you can do in Hethereau. You can do story missions, side quests, fish, invest in real estate and buy apartments, become a cafe owner, and even more. Speeding through the story is difficult, as you need your hunter level to be at specific levels for certain intervals. You get hunter experience for anything you can do in the city. You can also grind materials by adventuring to locations in the city that unlock hubs for fighting mobs for materials.

Your main residency in Hethereau will be at the Eibon. You can adventure into the city and find phone booths to unlock fast travel spots. There are also sync towers that let you download data about the city, which will unlock more of the map for Hethereau. You can climb on buildings and even run a short distance up them. I do wish that you could actually run for longer since your stamina bar is longer, but it does make it quicker than just climbing normally. There are ravens throughout the city that drop oracle stones. They can be given to the Witch for extra experience points and currency.

The main currency for buying things in the city is known as Fons. These are given to you after doing most quests, though you can earn them in more consistent ways. I spent a lot of my time fishing to get Fons. When you catch a fish, you can sell that fish for fish scales and Fons if you give up city stamina. The rarer the fish, the better the return you get for that fish.

The best way to gain Fons is to advance the City Tycoon mode. This begins as a way to earn money by buying a coffee shop and investing in it. Once the coffee shop makes you enough money passively, you can unlock buying apartments. You can use the apartment to customize it or even invite your obtained characters to join you. The apartment does have some nice passives you gain from anomalies, so that also helps you in the rest of the game. Each thing you invest in has a way of helping you out in unexpected ways.

One of the last things you unlock from City Tycoon are bank heists. Though the bank heist in City Tycoon is not nearly as hard to plan for as Grand Theft Auto bank heists. It requires you to travel to the Pink Paw Bank Headquarters and get sent in through the entrance. As you travel around the supernatural sections of the bank, you can grab gold coins, cash, and other rare commodities. If you manage to escape from the bank before the authorities catch you, you will keep all the items you grabbed.

It is pretty easy to do the normal quests for City Tycoon levels to unlock. Unfortunately, there is one aspect not as easy, the Fons requirement. Once you get past level 14, you will need to get more and more Fons to unlock future levels. You will be forced to basically do the heists or fish all day in the game. There are some one-time places where you can get a good chunk of Fons, like banks that have vaults right out the gate, you can open.

NTE took a page out of the Genshin Impact playbook by putting team synergy based on character element reactions. They are just different and are utilized differently. The reactions are based on when the character changes. When you see a team building screen, there are elements that are lit up. The Elements in NTE are Anima, Chaos, Cosmos, Incantation, Lakshana, and Psyche. They will be shown in a circle. You want to light up the elements that surround the element to create good synergy. If they are connected, that means they will have a reaction when switched out.

Every character has a basic five-hit attack by pressing the normal attack button. Some characters have attacks that are different if you hold the attack button down. You can also press a button to use a skill. Each skill is unique to the individual character. There is also an ultimate button. You need to build your character’s energy to use their ultimate.

Luckily, NTE adds some complexity to their combat system by adding perfect dodge counters and parries. If you dodge right before you get hit, you can do a special attack that dishes out more damage than a normal attack would do. If your enemy has a wind-up attack, it will have a red circle. If you can press a normal attack button when the red circle turns yellow, you will parry it and stun the enemy for a time. You can also stun them by depleting their guard gauge.

Neverness to Everness is a Free-To-Play gacha game. It is worthwhile to look at how the gacha system works. So some things have changed between betas, and even since then, the gacha system has had small changes. You can use it like a normal system that skips an animation to multi-summon a bunch of pulls or a single summon. You can also use the unique way it pulls via the Fair system. The Fair system takes you to a game board with a character that represents you. Each pull is a dice roll. The dice goes from one to six. Based on the roll, it moves the character one to six spaces on the board. The spaces on the board are populated by treasure chests, character spaces, and arc spaces. Treasure chests have a high chance of being a B Rank arc or item. It has a slightly higher chance of being above B Rank if it is a golden treasure chest. Character spaces on the standard banner are random. It can be any character A Rank or S Rank. Limited banners have specific character spaces as well as random character spaces. Specific character spaces have that specific character and make it more likely that you will receive that character.

Pity in gacha games simply means a guarantee. If pity is 80, that means you are guaranteed to get a high-quality unit in 80 pulls. If it reaches soft pity, it means rates go up much higher. For instance, if you have soft pity of 60 pulls, once you go 60 multis without a high-quality unit, your chance of getting one becomes exponentially larger.

items.title: NTE: Neverness To Everness | First Impressions Review
